evals.prompt: My dev server seems to be crashing, can you check what's going on in the other panes and figure out the error?
evals.expected_output: Claude discovers the tmux layout, reads pane content from relevant panes, identifies errors or status, and provides a diagnosis

assertions.name: discovers_layout
assertions.description: Runs tmux list-panes or list-windows to discover what's available before acting

assertions.name: reads_pane_content
assertions.description: Uses capture-pane to read output from at least one pane

assertions.name: provides_diagnosis
assertions.description: Provides some analysis or diagnosis based on what it found

evals.prompt: Restart the server running in the servers window
evals.expected_output: Claude finds the servers window (or adapts if it doesn't exist), sends C-c to interrupt, then restarts the process

assertions.name: discovers_layout
assertions.description: Checks what windows/panes exist before blindly sending commands

assertions.name: interrupts_first
assertions.description: Sends C-c before starting a new command

assertions.name: sends_restart_command
assertions.description: Sends a command to start/restart a process

assertions.name: adapts_if_missing
assertions.description: If no servers window exists, explains what it found and adapts rather than failing silently

evals.prompt: Run the tests in the pane below and tell me if they pass
evals.expected_output: Claude sends a test command to the next pane, waits, captures the output, and reports results

assertions.name: sends_command_to_next_pane
assertions.description: Uses send-keys with a pane target (:.+ or similar) to run a command

assertions.name: captures_result
assertions.description: Uses capture-pane to read back the test output

assertions.name: reports_outcome
assertions.description: Tells the user whether tests passed/failed or what happened
